project: The Impact of PMUY on Clean Fuel Adoption: A Difference-in-Differences Analysis Using NFHS-4 and NFHS-5 Data

sources.name: pmuy_data.csv
sources.note: Merged NFHS-4 and NFHS-5 household microdata. Primary outcome source.

sources.name: PPAC State-wise PMUY Connections
sources.note: Used only to validate high/low exposure split. Not a primary outcome source.

control_states: dadra and nagar haveli and daman and diu

success_threshold.rationale: Absolute-magnitude threshold: >=2.0 pp in either direction is policy-relevant. Naive DiD of -2.0 pp sets prior toward divergence. A significant negative result informs the Ministry refill-subsidy decision.

completed: Charter approved (CHARTER.md) — threshold |β̂₃| >= 2.0 pp, CI excludes zero
NFHS-4+5 panel loaded (1,238,208 raw rows; 1,235,952 after exclusions)
Outcome variable clean_fuel constructed from hv226
All binary controls constructed: rural, electricity, female_head, improved_water, improved_floor, piped_water, rich, head_higher_edu, wealth_quintile
35 states confirmed present in both NFHS-4 and NFHS-5
Treatment: high_exposure=1 for 17 low-access states below NFHS-4 median (52.3 pp)
Descriptive 2x2 summary table produced (4 group x period cells)
Naive DiD baseline: -2.0 pp written to outputs/baseline_metric.json
remaining_for_final: TWFE coefficient → outputs/primary_metric.json (run: uv run main.py)
report.md with results tables and event-study figure
template_warning: This scaffold runs, but it is not submission-ready until you replace the placeholders.
